title: Auto-trust captured decisions; surface conflicts inline, retire review queue
context: Review queue model for low-confidence (confidence<0.6) and proposed decisions created async backlog and friction — captured memory rots in draft state waiting for human triage. Touches invariant(s): con_2ce3f2a547 (Synthesis must use an explicitly selected coding-assistant subscription CLI or the deterministic local fallback; never call a pay-per-token API); con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Captured memory auto-enters graph as accepted advisory on landing (status→accepted). Safety preserved by keeping source=llm_draft (veto/strict gates key on source, not status, so it never blocks). Rare blocking conflicts surface as inline escalations asked within the session, not queued review items.
consequences: Captured memory grounds and ranks immediately with no async queue delay
Low-confidence memory is now weighted advisory, not a triage backlog item
Blocking authority still requires explicit human grant (unchanged)
Topic conflicts needing human decision are surfaced inline as questions, not deferred to batch review
Reduces friction and prevents decisions from rotting in review state
alternatives_rejected: Keep review queue but make it synchronous/blocking (would prevent capture)
Require human vouching before memory enters graph (too strict, kills advisory grounding)
Auto-trust without escalation mechanism (would miss topic conflicts needing human judgment)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep review queue but make it synchronous/blocking (would prevent capture)
Require human vouching before memory enters graph (too strict, kills advisory grounding)
Auto-trust without escalation mechanism (would miss topic conflicts needing human judgment)
